title: Explaining How to Use Something
learningGoal: The learner will verbally construct sentences using both the '~to' conditional for mechanical functions and the '~tara' conditional for hypothetical requests.
instructions: In this roleplay, a friend is asking for your help to use a machine, and then asks for a favor. Reply verbally in Japanese based on the English prompts. Remember when to use '~to' vs '~tara'.

contentItems.text: For the first prompt, use '~to' because it is a mechanical rule. For the others, use '~tara'.

speakingPrompt: Translate: 1) If you push the button, the door opens. 2) If you do not understand, please ask. 3) If you are free, please come.
scenario: A conversation between friends near a ticket machine.

subTasks.prompt: Explain the machine. Say: 'If you push the button, the door opens.'

sampleResponse.targetText: ボタンを おすと、ドアが あきます。
sampleResponse.transliteration: Botan wo osu to, doa ga akimasu.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: If you push the button, the door opens.

subTasks.prompt: Offer help. Say: 'If you do not understand, please ask.'

sampleResponse.targetText: わからなかったら、きいて ください。
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: If you do not understand, please ask.

subTasks.prompt: Invite them out. Say: 'If you are free, please come.'

sampleResponse.targetText: ひまだったら、きて ください。
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: If you are free, please come.

scenarioContext.targetText: この きかいは どうやって つかいますか。
scenarioContext.transliteration: Kono kikai wa dou yatte tsukaimasu ka.
scenarioContext.baseTranslation: How do you use this machine?

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: ボタンを おすと、ドアが あきます。
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Botan wo osu to, doa ga akimasu.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: If you push the button, the door opens.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: わからなかったら、きいて ください。
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: If you do not understand, please ask.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: ひまだったら、きて ください。
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: If you are free, please come.

expectedSpeakingPoints: Say Botan wo osu to, doa ga akimasu
acceptableParaphrases: Accept minor particle variations like 'doa wa akimasu'.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: ボタンを おすと、ドアが あきます。わからなかったら、きいて ください。ひまだったら、きて ください。

feedbackRules.onPass: Fantastic speaking! You perfectly juggled the two types of conditionals.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the negative tara-form. Wakaranai -> Wakaranakattara.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you didn't say 'Hima tara'. Because Hima is a na-adjective, it needs 'dattara'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: この きかいは どうやって つかいますか。ボタンを おすと、ドアが あきます。わからなかったら、きいて ください。ひまだったら、きて ください。

hintText: To say 'If you do not understand', change 'Wakaranai' to 'Wakaranakattara'.
commonMistakes: Saying 'Oshitara' instead of 'Osu to'. (Oshitara is grammatically okay but less natural for machines than Osu to).
